CBS News Live CBS News New York: Local News, Weather & More Nov 1, 2018 CBS News New York

Live Trump says "whole civilization will die tonight" as Iran deadline looms Iran's regime calls on civilians to shield power plants as Trump threatens "a whole civilization" with destruction if Tehran doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz. updated 1M ago

Some Rikers Island detainees to be moved to Manhattan hospital, Mamdani says Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced Tuesday that detainees at Rikers Island with complicated medical needs will be transferred to a Manhattan hospital to get access to speciality care. updated 1M ago

Gilgo Beach suspect Rex Heuermann expected to plead guilty Wednesday Rex Heuermann, the man accused of being the Gilgo Beach serial killer, is expected to plead guilty to multiple murders when he appears in court on Wednesday​. 1H ago

Vandal smashes windows of parked vehicles in Union County, N.J. Police in New Jersey are searching for a vandal who threw bricks through the windows of vehicles parked on streets in several Union County towns. 9H ago

U.S. journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnapping in Iraq, sources say American journalist Shelly Kittleson​ is being released on the condition that she leave Iraq immediately, an Iranian-backed militia in Iraq says. 3H ago
Stunning photos from Artemis II show Earth, moon, eclipse from space The astronauts aboard Artemis II are the first humans to see some parts of the far side of the moon with the naked eye. 7H ago

Video shows Old Westbury Police officers save man from sudden cardiac arrest Police officers in Nassau County are being lauded for their quick thinking after saving a diner patron who went into cardiac arrest last month. 22H ago

Latest Videos N.Y. budget proposal could roll back biomarker testing coverage, advocates say Three years ago, New York state took a huge step forward expanding access to biomarker testing, which is said to be a game changer in cancer care. Now, however, advocates fear the state could be taking a step back for those with Medicaid. CBS News New York's Jenna DeAngelis reports. 16M ago 02:02
Bomb Squad, FBI called to investigate boom at Yonkers park The Bomb Squad and FBI were called to investigate a mysterious loud boom coming from a park in Yonkers on Monday night. 23M ago 00:20

2 suspects indicted in fatal shooting of 7-month-old girl in Brooklyn The two suspects accused in the fatal shooting of a 7-month-old girl in Brooklyn were indicted Tuesday on multiple charges, including murder. CBS News New York's Lisa Rozner reports. 26M ago 02:02

Threats escalate in war with Iran as Trump issues ultimatum | Team coverage Talks in the war with Iran appear to have hit a stalemate as threats escalate. CBS News New York's Allen Devlin and Mahsa Saeidi report. 31M ago 05:22

"Cats: The Jellicle Ball," a reimagining of a beloved classic, opens Wednesday night The show is a re-imagining of Andrew Lloyd Webber's "Cats," blending New York City Black and Latin LGBTQ+ ballroom culture. It runs through September at the Broadhurst Theatre. CBS News New York's Cindy Hsu reports. 4H ago 00:25

First Alert Weather Very chilly weather for tonight across the New York-area It's a cold and breezy night with feels-like temperatures in the 20s, but warm weather will come back for the weekend. CBS News New York Meteorologist Tony Sadiku has the forecast. 5H ago 03:39

First Alert Weather 101: Have a meteorologist visit your classroom! From the science of forecasting to weather safety – First Alert Weather 101 is an interactive way to get students thinking about STEM. There's also a chance your students might be on TV. May 2, 2022

Mamdani administration releases 1st NYC racial equity plan, cost of living report Mayor Zohran Mamdani's administration released New York City's first Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan​ and True Cost of Living Measure​. 23H ago

NYC schools' class size reduction likely to be pushed back, senator says New York City's attempt to reduce class sizes will likely be pushed back for two years, a state senator told CBS News New York. Apr 5

Mayor Mamdani, Cardi B announce 2-K application date, citywide jingle competition NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani and rapper Cardi B announced Friday that 2-K applications will open in June. They also unveiled a jingle competition to build excitement for the program. Apr 3

NYC to roll out anti-street harassment ad campaign this week New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's office is set to roll out a new ad campaign this week targeting domestic and gender-based violence. Mar 31

Blakeman sets up camp at City Hall, criticizes Hochul and Mamdani New York's Republican nominee for governor Bruce Blakeman took his campaign to the steps of City Hall on Monday, where he portrayed Kathy Hochul and Mayor Zohran Mamdani as the taxing twins. Mar 30

Free child care center for city workers being built, Mayor Mamdani announces New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced municipal workers across the city will be able to get free child care at a new center being built. Mar 30

NYC Council passes bills allowing buffer zones around schools, houses of worship The New York City Council has passed legislation to allow the NYPD to set up buffer zones around schools and houses of worship. Mar 26

NYC pays the rent for an early education center in the Bronx. It sits empty At least 27 early childhood education centers across the city are vacant, NYC's deputy chancellor of the Division of Early Childhood Education said. Mar 25

2 NYC towing companies accused of overcharging still working with the city Two towing companies were forced to pay back thousands after City Hall said they overcharged New Yorkers last year, so how are they still able to tow cars on behalf of the city? Mar 12

New York stock traders sound alarm on spike in pump-and-dump schemes Data shows a massive increase in pump-and-dump schemes over the last year. Two traders from New York say it cost them tens of thousands of dollars. Mar 9

New York professor "over the moon" to help NASA's Artemis program Stony Brook University professor Timothy Glotch is one of 10 scientists selected to shape research plans for NASA's next Artemis missions. Apr 3

Chronic pain could lead seniors to misuse opioids, experts warn Older adults in New Jersey recorded the highest number of suspected overdose deaths in 2025, according to the New Jersey Department of Health​. Mar 16

Deadly opioid overdoes rate climbs among seniors as more get prescriptions for chronic pain Overdose deaths involving people 65 and older have increased by 400% during the last two decades, with a third of seniors misusing opioids prescribed to them for pain, according to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. CBS News New York's Jennifer Bisram reports. Mar 16 04:14

Increasing elder fraud a "huge concern," FBI and NYC officials say The FBI says older Americans lost $4.8 billion through fraud in 2024, with reports of theft through fraud​ jumping 43% in just one year. Mar 2

More older people being targeted with fraud schemes, FBI says The FBI says older Americans lost $4.8 billion lost through fraud in 2024, with reports of theft through fraud jumping 43% in just one year. CBS News New York's Jennifer Bisram reports. Mar 2 04:42

New Yorkers unretiring due to financial outlook, AARP says As the cost of living rises and some investments turn shaky, the financial outlook is forcing some New Yorkers to come out of retirement. Feb 13
